subject: Anthropology
title: Primate Social Behavior
description: Learn Primate Social Behavior in one minute.

hook.text: A chimp shares meat with allies but not rivals. A baboon grooms a partner to win favors. Sound familiar?

buildup.text: Primates live in complex social groups with hierarchies, alliances, conflicts, and reconciliation behaviors.

discovery.text: Grooming is the social glue. It reduces stress, builds trust, and maintains bonds — primate diplomacy in action.

twist.text: Some primates deceive, console, and show empathy. Social intelligence may have driven brain evolution more than tools.

climax.text: Primate social life reveals the roots of human politics. Our alliances, grudges, and empathy are ancient.

punchline.text: Human politics began in primate troops.

quiz.question: What role does grooming play in primate groups?
options: Building social bonds and maintaining alliances
